description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall constructed from roughly hewn, weathered blocks. The well is topped by a wooden canopy supported by two thick, vertical wooden posts, and a pitched roof made of dark, aged wooden planks. A horizontal metal rod runs between the posts, fitted with a hand crank on one side, suggesting a manual water-drawing mechanism. The interior of the well is open, with no visible cover, allowing access to the water below. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or modern enhancements.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden canopy, pitched roof, and hand crank mechanism. The structure is weathered and utilitarian, with no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open stone well, wooden posts, pitched roof, and the hand crank on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden post and the triangular profile of the roof, with the stone well visible below.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the open well, wooden posts, roof, and the hand crank now on the right side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the hand crank mechanism clearly visible in the center, and the stone well beneath the wooden structure.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal hand crank and rod mechanism is present, mounted horizontally between the wooden posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A circular stone wall surrounds the well, approximately waist-high, with a wooden canopy and roof supported by two posts.
